commands.review_summary:
The PR adds bounded preservation of action-critical command-output lines, threads a separate delivery summary through cron run events, and changes cron announce/webhook delivery to use compact non-interactive text.

PR surface: Source +226, Tests +114. Total +340 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main tail-truncates command stdout/stderr in runCommandWithTimeout, then runCronCommandJob summarizes only the truncated tail for cron results.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the branch has security-boundary findings, missing contributor real behavior proof, and competing open fix candidates for the same canonical issue.

target.title: fix(cron): preserve auth prompts in truncated command results
target.body:
﻿## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96343.

Cron command output can be truncated before an interactive auth/setup prompt reaches the visible result. That makes device-code login flows hard to complete because the operator may lose the login URL, code, or next-action instruction.

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR was rewritten as a minimal fix on top of current `main`. It keeps the normal command output cap, but lets the process runner preserve a small bounded set of important auth/setup lines when truncation happens.

The cron command runner uses those preserved lines only for the interactive/operator summary. Non-interactive delivery paths keep using a separate delivery summary so webhook and announce messages stay bounded and do not inherit the expanded interactive text.

## User Impact

- Interactive cron command results keep device-code/login prompts visible even when noisy command output is truncated.
- Non-critical noisy output is still truncated as before.
- Webhook/announce delivery continues to use the compact delivery summary rather than the interactive result summary.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/command-runner.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/process/exec.ts src/cron/command-runner.ts src/cron/types.ts src/cron/service/state.ts src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts src/gateway/server-cron.ts src/gateway/server-cron-notifications.ts src/cron/command-runner.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
